{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "94898060",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# 01 — Data Exploration (Week 2)\n",
    "\n",
    "**Goal**: Load multiple months of CRMLS Sold data, restrict to single-family residential, and explore key variable distributions.\n",
    "\n",
    "- Data: `data/sold/`\n",
    "- Column reference: `week1_data_note.md`\n",
    "- Checklist: `week2_checklist.md`\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n",
    "**Analysis log** (fill in once done):\n",
    "- Months used: TODO\n",
    "- Filter: PropertyType=Residential and PropertySubType=SingleFamilyResidence\n",
    "- Final sample size: TODO"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "801e4e60",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## A. Load data\n",
    "⚠️ `_filled` months and raw months have different column sets. Take the common columns BEFORE concatenating, otherwise you get all-NaN columns."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "b90481e8",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import glob\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "pd.set_option(\"display.max_columns\", 100)\n",
    "plt.rcParams[\"figure.figsize\"] = (8, 5)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "d5e2e533",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Choose which months to load: start with ~12 months to get it working, expand later if needed.\n",
    "# File names look like CRMLSSold202401_filled.csv / CRMLSSold202502.csv\n",
    "DATA_DIR = \"data/sold\"\n",
    "PATTERN = \"CRMLSSold202[45]*.csv\"  # TODO: adjust as needed (e.g. add 2026 or the 2022-2023 combined file)\n",
    "\n",
    "files = sorted(glob.glob(f\"{DATA_DIR}/{PATTERN}\"))\n",
    "print(f\"Matched {len(files)} files:\")\n",
    "for f in files:\n",
    "    print(\" \", f)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "ea5d2020",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Read each file, keep common columns, then concatenate; tag year_month for later monthly analysis.\n",
    "import re\n",
    "\n",
    "dfs = []\n",
    "for f in files:\n",
    "    d = pd.read_csv(f, low_memory=False)\n",
    "    ym = re.search(r\"(\\d{6})\", f.split(\"/\")[-1])  # grab YYYYMM from the file name\n",
    "    d[\"year_month\"] = ym.group(1) if ym else \"unknown\"\n",
    "    d[\"source_file\"] = f.split(\"/\")[-1]\n",
    "    dfs.append(d)\n",
    "\n",
    "common = set.intersection(*[set(d.columns) for d in dfs])\n",
    "print(f\"Common columns: {len(common)} (raw column counts per file: {[d.shape[1] for d in dfs]})\")\n",
    "\n",
    "df = pd.concat([d[list(common)] for d in dfs], ignore_index=True)\n",
    "print(f\"After merge: {df.shape[0]:,} rows x {df.shape[1]} cols\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "252b9c5d",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Sanity check: confirm no column came out all-NaN (the classic symptom of schema misalignment)\n",
    "df.head()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c3aeb736",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## B. Restrict to modeling scope (single-family residential)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "e0a693f3",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "before = len(df)\n",
    "df = df[(df[\"PropertyType\"] == \"Residential\") &\n",
    "        (df[\"PropertySubType\"] == \"SingleFamilyResidence\")].copy()\n",
    "print(f\"Before {before:,} rows -> after {len(df):,} rows (kept {len(df)/before*100:.1f}%)\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "809f313d",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## C. Key variable distributions\n",
    "Explore the 5 core variables one by one. See the hints in each cell."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "68edd319",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### C1. ClosePrice (target variable)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "3b65aa6a",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "count    2.692320e+05\n",
      "mean     1.289981e+06\n",
      "std      5.271615e+06\n",
      "min      0.000000e+00\n",
      "25%      6.250000e+05\n",
      "50%      8.950000e+05\n",
      "75%      1.435000e+06\n",
      "max      9.895000e+08\n",
      "Name: ClosePrice, dtype: float64\n",
      "Suspicious low (<10k): 5 | nulls: 2\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# TODO:\n",
    "# 1) df[\"ClosePrice\"].describe()\n",
    "# 2) Histogram: plt.hist(...)  -- likely right-skewed with a long tail\n",
    "# 3) Try a log transform: np.log1p(df[\"ClosePrice\"]) and plot again, check if it looks more normal\n",
    "cp = df[\"ClosePrice\"]\n",
    "print(cp.describe())\n",
    "print(\"Suspicious low (<10k):\", (cp < 1e4).sum(), \"| nulls:\", cp.isna().sum())\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))\n",
    "ax[0].hist(cp.dropna(), bins=60)\n",
    "ax[0].set_title(\"Raw ClosePrice (right-skewed long tail)\"); ax[0].set_xlabel(\"Close Price\")\n",
    "ax[1].hist(np.log1p(cp.dropna()), bins=60)\n",
    "ax[1].set_title(\"log1p(ClosePrice)\"); ax[1].set_xlabel(\"log(Close Price)\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c64211e1",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### C2. LivingArea"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 14,
   "id": "c0bafcd4",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "count    269088.000000\n",
      "mean       2030.361507\n",
      "std        1060.776538\n",
      "min           0.000000\n",
      "25%        1375.000000\n",
      "50%        1800.000000\n",
      "75%        2416.000000\n",
      "max      123764.000000\n",
      "Name: LivingArea, dtype: float64\n",
      "nulls: 146 | zeros: 112\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# TODO:\n",
    "# 1) Distribution (describe + histogram)\n",
    "# 2) Scatter vs ClosePrice to see the positive correlation\n",
    "la = df[\"LivingArea\"]\n",
    "print(la.describe())\n",
    "print(\"nulls:\", la.isna().sum(), \"| zeros:\", (la == 0).sum())\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))\n",
    "# 左：分布\n",
    "ax[0].hist(la.dropna(), bins=60)\n",
    "ax[0].set_title(\"LivingArea distribution\"); ax[0].set_xlabel(\"sqft\")\n",
    "sample = df[[\"LivingArea\", \"ClosePrice\"]].dropna().sample(min(5000, len(df)))\n",
    "ax[1].scatter(np.log(sample[\"LivingArea\"].clip(lower=1)),\n",
    "              np.log(sample[\"ClosePrice\"].clip(lower=1)), s=3, alpha=0.3)\n",
    "ax[1].set_title(\"log(LivingArea) vs log(ClosePrice)\")\n",
    "ax[1].set_xlabel(\"log sqft\"); ax[1].set_ylabel(\"log price\")\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c298d4ec",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### C3. BedroomsTotal"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 15,
   "id": "f27c935b",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "count    269234.000000\n",
      "mean          3.478446\n",
      "std           0.960194\n",
      "min           0.000000\n",
      "25%           3.000000\n",
      "50%           3.000000\n",
      "75%           4.000000\n",
      "max          45.000000\n",
      "Name: BedroomsTotal, dtype: float64\n",
      "nulls: 0 | zeros: 154 | >10: 43\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# TODO: distribution (value_counts / histogram); watch for outliers (e.g. 0 or extremely large)\n",
    "bd = df[\"BedroomsTotal\"]\n",
    "print(bd.describe())\n",
    "print(\"nulls:\", bd.isna().sum(), \"| zeros:\", (bd == 0).sum(), \"| >10:\", (bd > 10).sum())\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))\n",
    "# left: count bar chart (cap at a reasonable range to avoid the long error-tail)\n",
    "bd.value_counts().sort_index().loc[:10].plot(kind=\"bar\", ax=ax[0])\n",
    "ax[0].set_title(\"BedroomsTotal counts\"); ax[0].set_xlabel(\"bedrooms\")\n",
    "# right: median price by bedroom count -- the predictive signal\n",
    "df[df.BedroomsTotal <= 10].groupby(\"BedroomsTotal\")[\"ClosePrice\"].median().plot(marker=\"o\", ax=ax[1])\n",
    "ax[1].set_title(\"Median ClosePrice by bedrooms\"); ax[1].set_ylabel(\"median price\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "418660ff",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### C4. BathroomsTotalInteger"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "f2fd3748",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "count    269185.000000\n",
      "mean          2.611958\n",
      "std           1.208279\n",
      "min           0.000000\n",
      "25%           2.000000\n",
      "50%           2.000000\n",
      "75%           3.000000\n",
      "max         175.000000\n",
      "Name: BathroomsTotalInteger, dtype: float64\n",
      "nulls: 49 | zeros: 115 | >10: 153\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# TODO: distribution\n",
    "bt = df[\"BathroomsTotalInteger\"]         \n",
    "print(bt.describe())\n",
    "print(\"nulls:\", bt.isna().sum(), \"| zeros:\", (bt == 0).sum(), \"| >10:\", (bt > 10).sum())\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))\n",
    "bt.value_counts().sort_index().loc[:10].plot(kind=\"bar\", ax=ax[0])\n",
    "ax[0].set_title(\"Bathrooms counts\"); ax[0].set_xlabel(\"bathrooms\")\n",
    "df[df.BathroomsTotalInteger <= 10].groupby(\"BathroomsTotalInteger\")[\"ClosePrice\"].median().plot(marker=\"o\", ax=ax[1])\n",
    "#                       \n",
    "ax[1].set_title(\"Median ClosePrice by bathrooms\"); ax[1].set_ylabel(\"median price\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()\n",
    "\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "1d363f5b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### C5. LotSize\n",
    "Pick one of `LotSizeSquareFeet` or `LotSizeAcres` (don't use both). Usually heavily right-skewed."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 26,
   "id": "61453c31",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "count    2.646610e+05\n",
      "mean     2.462326e+05\n",
      "std      1.353201e+07\n",
      "min      0.000000e+00\n",
      "25%      5.663000e+03\n",
      "50%      7.245000e+03\n",
      "75%      1.032000e+04\n",
      "max      2.087221e+09\n",
      "Name: LotSizeSquareFeet, dtype: float64\n",
      "nulls: 4573 | zeros: 130\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# TODO: pick one LotSize column, look at the distribution (may need log or clipping of extreme values)\n",
    "ls = df[\"LotSizeSquareFeet\"]\n",
    "print(ls.describe())\n",
    "print(\"nulls:\", ls.isna().sum(), \"| zeros:\", (ls == 0).sum())\n",
    "\n",
    "valid = df[(df.LotSizeSquareFeet > 0) & (df.ClosePrice > 0)]\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))\n",
    "# left: log distribution (raw is unplottable due to the 2-billion outlier)\n",
    "ax[0].hist(np.log1p(ls[ls > 0]), bins=60)\n",
    "ax[0].set_title(\"log1p(LotSizeSquareFeet)\"); ax[0].set_xlabel(\"log sqft\")\n",
    "# right: log-log scatter vs price -- expect a weak/flat cloud\n",
    "s = valid.sample(min(5000, len(valid)), random_state=42)\n",
    "ax[1].scatter(np.log(s.LotSizeSquareFeet), np.log(s.ClosePrice), s=3, alpha=0.3)\n",
    "ax[1].set_title(\"log Lot vs log Price (weak)\"); ax[1].set_xlabel(\"log sqft\"); ax[1].set_ylabel(\"log price\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 27,
   "id": "cdffdaf7",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "ClosePrice               0.0\n",
       "LivingArea               0.1\n",
       "BedroomsTotal            0.0\n",
       "BathroomsTotalInteger    0.0\n",
       "LotSizeSquareFeet        1.7\n",
       "LotSizeAcres             1.7\n",
       "dtype: float64"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 27,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Null rate of key variables (per note: Living/Bed/Bath ~5-7%, LotSize ~9-10%)\n",
    "key_cols = [\"ClosePrice\", \"LivingArea\", \"BedroomsTotal\",\n",
    "            \"BathroomsTotalInteger\", \"LotSizeSquareFeet\", \"LotSizeAcres\"]\n",
    "(df[key_cols].isna().mean() * 100).round(1)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "18537b96",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## D. Outliers / correlation"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 30,
   "id": "c2ef1ed9",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "=== Outlier check (counts) ===\n",
      "ClosePrice <10k or >50M: 30\n",
      "LivingArea <200 or >20000: 140\n",
      "Bedrooms >10: 43 | Bathrooms >10: 153\n",
      "LotSizeSqFt <=0 or >5acre(217800): 3357\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 800x600 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# TODO:\n",
    "# - ClosePrice outliers: very low (e.g. <10k, likely non-market transactions) / very high\n",
    "# - LivingArea / LotSize = 0 or unreasonably large\n",
    "# - Correlation heatmap of numeric columns vs ClosePrice: df[num_cols].corr()\n",
    "print(\"=== Outlier check (counts) ===\")\n",
    "print(\"ClosePrice <10k or >50M:\", ((df.ClosePrice<1e4)|(df.ClosePrice>5e7)).sum())\n",
    "print(\"LivingArea <200 or >20000:\", ((df.LivingArea<200)|(df.LivingArea>20000)).sum())\n",
    "print(\"Bedrooms >10:\", (df.BedroomsTotal>10).sum(), \"| Bathrooms >10:\", (df.BathroomsTotalInteger>10).sum())\n",
    "print(\"LotSizeSqFt <=0 or >5acre(217800):\", ((df.LotSizeSquareFeet<=0)|(df.LotSizeSquareFeet>217800)).sum())\n",
    "feats = [\"ClosePrice\",\"LivingArea\",\"BedroomsTotal\",\"BathroomsTotalInteger\",\n",
    "         \"LotSizeSquareFeet\",\"GarageSpaces\",\"Stories\",\"YearBuilt\"]\n",
    "L = df[feats].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors=\"coerce\")\n",
    "\n",
    "for c in [\"ClosePrice\",\"LivingArea\",\"LotSizeSquareFeet\"]:\n",
    "    L[c] = np.log1p(L[c].clip(lower=0))\n",
    "corr = L.corr()\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,6))\n",
    "im = ax.imshow(corr, cmap=\"coolwarm\", vmin=-1, vmax=1)\n",
    "ax.set_xticks(range(len(feats))); ax.set_xticklabels(feats, rotation=45, ha=\"right\")\n",
    "ax.set_yticks(range(len(feats))); ax.set_yticklabels(feats)\n",
    "for i in range(len(feats)):\n",
    "    for j in range(len(feats)):\n",
    "        ax.text(j, i, f\"{corr.iloc[i,j]:.2f}\", ha=\"center\", va=\"center\", fontsize=8)\n",
    "plt.colorbar(im); plt.title(\"Correlation (log-space)\"); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a02c5df6",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## E. Time dimension (bonus, sets up the Week 3 training-window choice X)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 31,
   "id": "937f68ad",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# TODO: look at median ClosePrice by year_month over time (any price drift / trend?)\n",
    "# df.groupby(\"year_month\")[\"ClosePrice\"].median().plot(marker=\"o\")\n",
    "g = (df[(df.ClosePrice > 1e4) & (df.ClosePrice < 5e7)]\n",
    "     .groupby(\"year_month\")[\"ClosePrice\"].agg([\"median\", \"count\"]))\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 4))\n",
    "ax1.plot(g.index, g[\"median\"], marker=\"o\", color=\"tab:blue\", label=\"median price\")\n",
    "ax1.set_ylabel(\"median ClosePrice\", color=\"tab:blue\")\n",
    "ax1.tick_params(axis=\"x\", rotation=90)\n",
    "ax2 = ax1.twinx()\n",
    "ax2.bar(g.index, g[\"count\"], alpha=0.2, color=\"tab:gray\")\n",
    "ax2.set_ylabel(\"# sales\", color=\"tab:gray\")\n",
    "ax1.set_title(\"Median ClosePrice & volume by month\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "4c395621",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## F. Summary of key findings (for Week 3)\n",
    "After filling in the above, summarize a few bullet points here:\n",
    "- ClosePrice distribution shape / whether a log transform is needed: TODO\n",
    "- Key feature missingness & handling plan: TODO\n",
    "- Outlier rules discovered: TODO\n",
    "- Features most correlated with ClosePrice: TODO\n",
    "- Whether price drifts over time (affects training window X): TODO"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "metadata": {
  "kernelspec": {
   "display_name": "Python 3",
   "language": "python",
   "name": "python3"
  },
  "language_info": {
   "name": "python",
   "version": "3.11.7"
  }
 },
 "nbformat": 4,
 "nbformat_minor": 5
}
